Title: The retrograde signaling protein GUN1 regulates tetrapyrrole biosynthesis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; gun1-1; gun1-103; GUN1ox1; GUN1ox2; gun1-102 complementation lines A3022 and A3026
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: tetrapyrroles support chloroplast biogenesis and plastid-to-nucleus retrograde signaling
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PPR protein with small MutS-related (SMR) domain
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly degraded in white light; accumulates at very young stages of leaf development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dark-grown seedlings; detectable mainly at very young leaf developmental stages
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark growth; white light; 5-aminolevulinic acid feeding; norflurazon; lincomycin
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gun1 mutant analysis; overexpression; complementation with FLAG-tagged GUN1 under native promoter; tetrapyrrole metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR; recombinant protein hemin-binding assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restricts flux through tetrapyrrole biosynthesis, reducing protochlorophyllide and total heme accumulation; enhances FC1 chelatase activity; promotes stronger repression of nuclear photosynthetic genes under NF when overexpressed
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. gun1 mutants accumulated more protochlorophyllide and heme, whereas GUN1 overexpressors accumulated less, showing that GUN1 restricts tetrapyrrole pathway flux. 2. GUN1 binds heme, Mg-protoporphyrin, Zn-protoporphyrin, and protoporphyrin IX, mainly through its PPR-containing region. 3. Native-promoter FLAG-GUN1 from plant extracts bound hemin, supporting physiological porphyrin binding. 4. Changes in GUN1 abundance correlated with altered HEMA1 and LHCB2.1 expression after norflurazon treatment, linking tetrapyrrole metabolism to retrograde signaling.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduced heme and protochlorophyllide and strengthened inhibition of HEMA1 and LHCB2.1 expression under NF; mutant overaccumulation of Pchlide may impair greening under some conditions
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GUN1 promoter for FLAG complementation lines; overexpression promoter not specified in provided text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate tetrapyrrole flux and plastid retrograde signaling during seedling establishment, but manipulation may perturb chloroplast development and photosynthetic gene expression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although GUN1 affects metabolism causally, it is not a pathway enzyme or canonical transcription factor; therefore classified as Common Gene. The paper proposes dual functions: restricting total tetrapyrrole synthesis while promoting FC1 activity. Full-length recombinant protein could not be expressed; truncated GUN1-PS was used for biochemical assays.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: cch mutant line cchZ 3-17 expressing FLAG-tagged GUN5
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: chlorophyll/tetrapyrrole biosynthesis and plastid signaling context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: FLAG-tagged control line in hemin-binding assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Displayed only faint hemin binding compared with GUN1 in plant extract assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLAG-tagged GUN5 was used as a control in plant extract hemin-binding assays. 2. Compared with GUN1, GUN5 showed only very faint binding to hemin beads under the assay conditions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was directly tested in Results as an experimental comparator, but the paper's main causal nutrient-metabolism subject is GUN1.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Recombinant protein assay; Arabidopsis seedling context
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: protoporphyrin IX; Zn2+ in assay, physiologically Fe2+ for heme synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Zn-protoporphyrin IX in assay; heme in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Chelates a metal ion into protoporphyrin IX; physiologically inserts ferrous iron to form heme, while the assay monitored zinc insertion to form Zn-protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control point for FC1-dependent heme signal, but not established here as overall pathway rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assay with recombinant protein; dark-grown seedling retrograde signaling context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant GST-FC1 enzyme assay; Michaelis-Menten kinetics; GUN1-PS addition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: GUN1 stimulated FC1 Zn-chelatase activity by decreasing the apparent Km for Proto IX from 26.5 µM to 4.5 µM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant Arabidopsis FC1 exhibited Zn-chelatase activity in vitro. 2. Addition of GUN1-PS enhanced FC1 activity linearly with increasing GUN1-PS concentration. 3. Kinetic analysis showed reduced apparent Km for Proto IX, indicating enhanced substrate affinity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Despite stimulation of FC1 activity in vitro, GUN1 overexpression reduced total heme in vivo, implying context-dependent effects on overall pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling plastids and extraplastidic heme pool discussed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Heme is proposed to mediate positive plastid-to-nucleus retrograde signaling and supports cellular heme-dependent processes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering node to alter heme-mediated retrograde signaling, but manipulation may affect chloroplast development and nuclear photosynthetic gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction with GUN1 is reported as specific to FC1, not FC2. In vitro assay used Zn-chelation as a standard proxy for ferrochelatase activity.
